text.title: Value Stream Management — Tools, Services, and References
text.summary: CLI tools, SaaS services, best practices, and key references for VSM instrumentation and DORA measurement.

list.item: four-keys (Google) — DORA pipeline reference implementation (https://github.com/dora-team/fourkeys)
gh api — GitHub deployments + PR timestamps for DORA collection
jq — stream join JSON across tools; essential for cross-tool ID linking (https://jqlang.github.io/jq/)
git log --pretty — cycle time inputs from commit timestamps (core git)
pagerduty-cli — MTTR data from incident logs (https://github.com/martindstone/pagerduty-cli)
glab api — GitLab deployment events, MR timestamps
LinearB — engineering metrics, GitHub-native, REST API; good for mid-size orgs (SaaS).
Plandek — Flow + DORA dashboards, REST API; supports multiple source integrations (SaaS).
Jellyfish — engineering benchmarking, REST API; strong on team-level analysis (SaaS).
Faros AI — DORA + Flow with open data layer; can self-host (SaaS/OSS).
Apache DevLake — self-host DORA + Flow with plugin architecture for all major tools (OSS).
Tasktop / Planview Viz — enterprise VSM platform; requires license; REST API partial (SaaS).
Code Climate Velocity — cycle time, PR throughput; GitHub-centric (SaaS).
Allstacks — forecasting from flow data; REST API (SaaS).

rule.statement: Visualize flow with cumulative flow diagram (CFD), not bar charts — CFD reveals stage starvation and WIP accumulation that bar charts hide.
rule.rationale: Bar charts show throughput at a point in time; CFDs show the rate of item entry and exit per stage, making bottleneck dynamics visible as widening bands.

rule.statement: Use Little's Law (WIP = Throughput × Cycle Time) as a sanity check on agent-collected data — large deviations indicate instrumentation errors.
rule.rationale: Little's Law holds for any stable system; if agent-collected WIP, throughput, and cycle time do not satisfy it, there is a data collection bug, not an anomaly.

rule.statement: When AI tooling lifts throughput, watch Change Failure Rate and rework rate — productivity that creates incidents is disguised debt.
rule.rationale: The AI productivity paradox: AI-generated code that deploys fast but fails in production increases MTTR and CFR, erasing the DF gain. Track the full four-tuple together.

list.item: Mik Kersten, "Project to Product" (2018) — Flow Framework foundational text
Forsgren, Humble, Kim, "Accelerate" (2018) — DORA research and four-tuple
Goldratt, "The Goal" (1984) — Theory of Constraints
Google Cloud DORA report 2024 — https://cloud.google.com/devops/state-of-devops
Apache DevLake — https://devlake.apache.org
Sibling methodologies: earned-value-management/, agile-ceremonies-setup/, pm-tool-selection/, schedule-development/
